title: GROUP_SEND: no max payload size check — 1 MiB frame fans out to 100 members
description: GROUP_SEND checks empty-payload but has no upper size limit. MAX_WS_MESSAGE_BYTES=1 MiB bounds inbound frames. A subscribed user can send a 1 MiB GROUP_SEND; the relay fans it out to all group members via broadcast_to_group. With MAX_GROUP_MEMBERS=100 that is 100 serialized copies of a 1 MiB payload queued into 100 mpsc channels, plus offline_messages inserts for offline members. One frame causes O(N*payload) memory allocation. A per-message size cap (e.g. 256 KiB for GROUP_SEND) would bound the blast radius. BROADCAST already caps at 1 MiB at the frame level — GROUP_SEND should apply a lower per-group-message cap.
